title: tetranthera nervosa var. coriacea
intro: Tetranthera nervosa var. coriacea, commonly known as the New Zealand tree daphne, is a plant species native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Tetranthera nervosa var. coriacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The plant can be used to prepare various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
